sections.paragraph:
σπαργανόω (fr. *σπάργω via σπάργανον [‘swathing-band’], found mostly in the pl. σπάργανα [M-M s.v. σπαργανόω]; Eur., Hippocr.+; Posidon.: 87 Fgm. 58a Jac.; Plut.; Ps.-Apollod. 1, 1, 7; Job 38:9; Ezk 16:4; Vi. Elij. 2 [p. 93, 10f Sch.]) 1 aor. ἐσπαργάνωσα; pf. pass. ptc. ἐσπαργανωμένος (Cornutus 6 p. 7, 7) to wrap in pieces of cloth used for swaddling infants, wrap up in cloths τινά someone Lk 2:7 (GJs 22:2). Pass. Lk 2:12 (s. Goodsp, Probs. 73f: ‘wrapped him up’ vs. 7, ‘a baby wrapped up’ vs. 12).—DELG s.v. *σπάργω.

  1. fr. = from
  2. pl. = plural

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Eur Eur , V b.c.—List 5

Hippocr Hippocr = Corpus Hippocraticum, V–IV b.c.—List 5

Posidon Posidon , II–I b.c.—List 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

Jac. Jac. = Die Fragmente der griechischen Historiker, ed. FJacoby—Lists 5, 6

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

Ps.-Apollod Ps.-Apollod , myth., I/II a.d.—List 5

Vi. Vi. = Vita, Vitae

Sch. Sch. = TSchermann, Prophetarum vitae fabulosae—List 5

aor. aor. = aorist

  1. pf. = perfect

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

ptc. ptc. = participle

Cornutus Cornutus , I a.d.—List 5

GJs GJs = Gospel of James (Protevangelium Jacobi), II a.d.—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 936
